title: frangula rupestris
intro: Frangula rupestris, also known as Wall barberry, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the family Rhamnaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anthraquinone glycosides, which have laxative properties, making it a natural remedy for constipation and other digestive issues. The health benefits of Frangula rupestris include its ability to regulate bowel movements, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Frangula rupestris can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried root extract, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
